conflicts.stored_value: A recorded audition does not replace prescreening.

Prescreen: Two movements of an unaccompanied sonata by Bach; First movement of one listed concerto; A virtuoso concert piece at the level of Paganini, Sarasate, Vieuxtemps, Wieniawski, or Ysaÿe. Audition: First and second movements from an unaccompanied sonata by Bach; First movement of one listed concerto; A virtuoso concert piece at the level of Paganini, Sarasate, Vieuxtemps, Wieniawski, or Ysaÿe.
conflicts.incoming_value: Two movements of unaccompanied Bach, the first movement of a listed major concerto, and a virtuoso work.
Bachelor of Music in Classical – Violin

before.repertoire_summary: Prescreen: two contrasting concerto movements and two contrasting solo Bach movements. Final audition: a complete concerto plus four significant recital works spanning broad stylistic periods, including the 20th or 21st century.
before.conditional_notes: The final audition must be in person.

after.repertoire_summary: A complete concerto and four full recital works including twentieth- or twenty-first-century music.

before.repertoire_summary: Prescreen: one concerto movement and two contrasting solo Bach movements. Final audition: a concerto movement or approved extended work, two contrasting Bach movements, and one work of the applicant’s choice.
before.conditional_notes: The final audition may be completed in person or by recording.

after.repertoire_summary: A concerto movement or movements with cadenza, unaccompanied Bach, and contrasting repertoire; MM adds a complete sonata and complete twentieth- or twenty-first-century work.

before.repertoire_summary: Prescreen: two contrasting concerto movements and two contrasting solo Bach movements. Final audition: a complete concerto plus four significant recital works spanning broad stylistic periods, including the 20th or 21st century.
before.conditional_notes: The final audition must be in person.

after.repertoire_summary: A complete concerto and four full recital works including twentieth- or twenty-first-century music.

before.repertoire_summary: Prescreen: two contrasting concerto movements and two contrasting solo Bach movements. Final audition: two concerto movements, three Bach movements, a complete sonata, and a complete 20th- or 21st-century work.
before.conditional_notes: The final audition may be completed in person or by recording.

after.repertoire_summary: A concerto movement or movements with cadenza, unaccompanied Bach, and contrasting repertoire; MM adds a complete sonata and complete twentieth- or twenty-first-century work.

before.repertoire_summary: Prescreen portfolio: three to five original contrasting compositions with scores and recordings. Final round: composition project, faculty interview, and portfolio review.
before.conditional_notes: BM and MM faculty interviews may be in person or by Zoom; the composition audition has prescreen, project, and interview/portfolio stages.

after.repertoire_summary: Prescreen portfolio of three to five original contrasting compositions; invited applicants submit an updated portfolio and complete the assigned composition audition project.

before.repertoire_summary: Prescreen portfolio: three to five original contrasting compositions with scores and recordings. Final round: composition project, an in-person faculty interview, and portfolio review.
before.conditional_notes: DMA applicants must interview in person; the composition audition has prescreen, project, and interview/portfolio stages.

after.repertoire_summary: Prescreen portfolio of three to five original contrasting compositions; invited applicants submit an updated portfolio and complete the assigned composition audition project.

before.repertoire_summary: Prescreen portfolio: three to five original contrasting compositions with scores and recordings. Final round: composition project, faculty interview, and portfolio review.
before.conditional_notes: BM and MM faculty interviews may be in person or by Zoom; the composition audition has prescreen, project, and interview/portfolio stages.

after.repertoire_summary: Prescreen portfolio of three to five original contrasting compositions; invited applicants submit an updated portfolio and complete the assigned composition audition project.

before.repertoire_summary: Prescreen: a 60–70 minute solo recital representing at least three stylistic periods. Final audition: a 60–75 minute solo recital representing at least three stylistic periods.
before.conditional_notes: The final audition must be in person.

after.repertoire_summary: A sixty- to seventy-five-minute recital spanning at least three periods, performed from memory.

before.repertoire_summary: Prescreen: at least two contrasting works totaling 15–20 minutes. Final audition: Baroque repertoire, a complete Classical sonata, a 19th-century work, and a 20th- or 21st-century work.
before.conditional_notes: The final audition may be completed in person or by recording.

after.repertoire_summary: Baroque, complete Classical sonata, nineteenth-century, and twentieth- or twenty-first-century works, performed from memory.

before.repertoire_summary: Prescreen: a 60–70 minute solo recital representing at least three stylistic periods. Final audition: a 60–75 minute solo recital representing at least three stylistic periods.
before.conditional_notes: The final audition must be in person.

after.repertoire_summary: A sixty- to seventy-five-minute recital spanning at least three periods, performed from memory.

before.repertoire_summary: Prescreen: at least two contrasting works totaling 15–20 minutes. Final audition: a 50–65 minute solo recital covering at least three stylistic periods, including the 20th or 21st century.
before.conditional_notes: The final audition may be completed in person or by recording.

after.repertoire_summary: A fifty- to sixty-five-minute recital spanning at least three periods and including twentieth- or twenty-first-century music, performed from memory.

before.repertoire_summary: Prescreen: two contrasting concerto movements, one sonata movement, and one work of the applicant’s choice. Final audition: a complete concerto and four complete recital works spanning broad stylistic periods, including the 20th or 21st century.
before.conditional_notes: The final audition must be in person.

after.repertoire_summary: A complete concerto with cadenzas and four full recital works from contrasting periods including twentieth- or twenty-first-century music.

before.repertoire_summary: Prescreen: A full-length recital of works from contrasting style periods. Audition: A full-length recital of works from contrasting style periods.
before.conditional_notes: Final auditions may be in person or recorded for BM and MM; DMA and AD require in-person auditions.

after.repertoire_summary: A complete recital program of contrasting works suitable for advanced study.

before.repertoire_summary: Prescreen: One movement of a concerto; Two contrasting movements of solo Bach. Audition: Three-octave major and minor scales and arpeggios; First movement of a concerto with applicable cadenza; Two contrasting movements of a Bach partita or sonata; A virtuoso concert piece.
before.conditional_notes: A recorded audition does not replace prescreening.

after.repertoire_summary: A major concerto movement or movements with cadenza, unaccompanied Bach, and a virtuoso work; BM also presents scales and arpeggios, and MM adds a sonata movement.

before.repertoire_summary: Prescreen: Two movements of a concerto; One movement of a sonata; One movement of a work of the applicant's choice. Audition: Complete concerto with all applicable cadenzas; Four significant recital works in their entirety from a wide range of style periods, including the 20th or 21st century.
before.conditional_notes: Final auditions may be in person or recorded for BM and MM; DMA and AD require in-person auditions.

after.repertoire_summary: A complete concerto with cadenzas and four full recital works from contrasting periods including twentieth- or twenty-first-century music.

before.repertoire_summary: Prescreen: A full-length recital of works from contrasting style periods. Audition: A full-length recital of works from contrasting style periods.
before.conditional_notes: Final auditions may be in person or recorded for BM and MM; DMA and AD require in-person auditions.

after.repertoire_summary: A complete recital program of contrasting works suitable for advanced study.

before.repertoire_summary: Prescreen: Two movements of a concerto; Two contrasting movements of solo Bach; One movement of a work of the applicant's choice. Audition: Two contrasting movements of a concerto including all applicable cadenzas; Two movements of an unaccompanied Bach partita or sonata; A virtuoso concert piece; One movement of a sonata.
before.conditional_notes: A recorded audition does not replace prescreening.

after.repertoire_summary: A major concerto movement or movements with cadenza, unaccompanied Bach, and a virtuoso work; BM also presents scales and arpeggios, and MM adds a sonata movement.

before.repertoire_summary: Prescreen: Two movements of an unaccompanied sonata by Bach; Two movements of one listed concerto; A virtuoso concert piece at the level of Paganini, Sarasate, Vieuxtemps, Wieniawski, or Ysaÿe. Audition: First and second movements from an unaccompanied sonata by Bach; Two movements of one listed concerto; A virtuoso concert piece at the level of Paganini, Sarasate, Vieuxtemps, Wieniawski, or Ysaÿe.
before.conditional_notes: A recorded audition does not replace prescreening.

after.repertoire_summary: Two movements of unaccompanied Bach, two movements of a listed major concerto, and a virtuoso work.

before.repertoire_summary: Prescreen: A combined package representing Italian, German, French, and English; Two operatic arias in different languages, period styles, and tempi/moods; one may be replaced by a concert or oratorio aria; Four art songs spanning different languages, periods, and tempi/moods; At least one underrepresented creator is strongly encouraged; a living composer is encouraged when suitable for classical singing. Audition: The same combined repertoire package as the prescreen.
before.conditional_notes: Final auditions may be in person or recorded for BM and MM; DMA and AD require in-person auditions.

after.repertoire_summary: Two contrasting operatic arias and four art songs collectively covering Italian, German, French, and English, performed from memory.

before.repertoire_summary: Prescreen: Three songs: one Italian song from the 17th or 18th century; one song in English that is not a translation; and one choice song in French, German, a native language other than English or Italian, or by a historically underrepresented creator. Audition: The same three-song repertoire as the prescreen.
before.conditional_notes: A recorded audition does not replace prescreening.

after.repertoire_summary: Three memorized songs: seventeenth- or eighteenth-century Italian, English in the original language, and a contrasting French, German, native-language, or underrepresented-composer selection.

before.repertoire_summary: Prescreen: A combined package representing Italian, German, French, and English; Two operatic arias in different languages, period styles, and tempi/moods; one may be replaced by a concert or oratorio aria; Four art songs spanning different languages, periods, and tempi/moods; At least one underrepresented creator is strongly encouraged; a living composer is encouraged when suitable for classical singing. Audition: The same combined repertoire package as the prescreen.
before.conditional_notes: Final auditions may be in person or recorded for BM and MM; DMA and AD require in-person auditions.

after.repertoire_summary: Two contrasting operatic arias and four art songs collectively covering Italian, German, French, and English, performed from memory.

before.repertoire_summary: Prescreen: Seven songs/arias: one song each in English, German, French, and Italian; one operatic aria; an oratorio aria or second operatic aria; and one choice selection; No more than two arias; an aria may count as the aria or language art-song selection but not both; each selection should be no more than four minutes. Audition: The same seven-piece repertoire as the prescreen; Be prepared to introduce yourself and discuss correct pronunciation and the composer/librettist's contribution to the vocal canon.
before.conditional_notes: A recorded audition does not replace prescreening.

after.repertoire_summary: Seven memorized songs and arias covering English, German, French, Italian, opera, oratorio or a second opera aria, and a native-language or underrepresented-composer selection.

after.source_quote: The Duolingo English Test is the only exam accepted; TOEFL and IELTS are not accepted. There is no minimum score required to apply.
All students who audition and are admitted to MSM are automatically considered for scholarships.

before.source_quote: DMA & AD programs require an in-person audition.

before.source_quote: Zukerman Violin applicants must submit prescreen recordings to be considered for an invitation to the final audition round.

after.source_quote: Prescreening is required for this major.

before.source_quote: Prescreen: A full-length recital of works from contrasting style periods. Audition: A full-length recital of works from contrasting style periods.

after.source_quote: A complete recital program of contrasting works suitable for advanced study.

before.source_quote: Classical Violin applicants must submit prescreen recordings to be considered for an invitation to the final audition round.

after.source_quote: Prescreening is required for this major.

before.source_quote: Prescreen: One movement of a concerto; Two contrasting movements of solo Bach. Audition: Three-octave major and minor scales and arpeggios; First movement of a concerto with applicable cadenza; Two contrasting movements of a Bach partita or sonata; A virtuoso concert piece.

after.source_quote: A major concerto movement or movements with cadenza, unaccompanied Bach, and a virtuoso work; BM also presents scales and arpeggios, and MM adds a sonata movement.

before.source_quote: Zukerman Violin applicants must submit prescreen recordings to be considered for an invitation to the final audition round.

after.source_quote: Prescreening is required for this major.

before.source_quote: Prescreen: Two movements of an unaccompanied sonata by Bach; First movement of one listed concerto; A virtuoso concert piece at the level of Paganini, Sarasate, Vieuxtemps, Wieniawski, or Ysaÿe. Audition: First and second movements from an unaccompanied sonata by Bach; First movement of one listed concerto; A virtuoso concert piece at the level of Paganini, Sarasate, Vieuxtemps, Wieniawski, or Ysaÿe.

after.source_quote: Two movements of unaccompanied Bach, the first movement of a listed major concerto, and a virtuoso work.

before.source_quote: Classical Violin applicants must submit prescreen recordings to be considered for an invitation to the final audition round.

after.source_quote: Prescreening is required for this major.

before.source_quote: Prescreen: Two movements of a concerto; One movement of a sonata; One movement of a work of the applicant's choice. Audition: Complete concerto with all applicable cadenzas; Four significant recital works in their entirety from a wide range of style periods, including the 20th or 21st century.

after.source_quote: A complete concerto with cadenzas and four full recital works from contrasting periods including twentieth- or twenty-first-century music.

before.source_quote: Zukerman Violin applicants must submit prescreen recordings to be considered for an invitation to the final audition round.

after.source_quote: Prescreening is required for this major.

before.source_quote: Prescreen: Two movements of a concerto; Two contrasting movements of solo Bach; One movement of a work of the applicant's choice. Audition: Two contrasting movements of a concerto including all applicable cadenzas; Two movements of an unaccompanied Bach partita or sonata; A virtuoso concert piece; One movement of a sonata.

after.source_quote: A major concerto movement or movements with cadenza, unaccompanied Bach, and a virtuoso work; BM also presents scales and arpeggios, and MM adds a sonata movement.

before.source_quote: Prescreen: Two movements of an unaccompanied sonata by Bach; Two movements of one listed concerto; A virtuoso concert piece at the level of Paganini, Sarasate, Vieuxtemps, Wieniawski, or Ysaÿe. Audition: First and second movements from an unaccompanied sonata by Bach; Two movements of one listed concerto; A virtuoso concert piece at the level of Paganini, Sarasate, Vieuxtemps, Wieniawski, or Ysaÿe.

after.source_quote: Two movements of unaccompanied Bach, two movements of a listed major concerto, and a virtuoso work.

before.source_quote: Vocal Arts applicants must submit prescreen recordings to be considered for an invitation to the final audition round.

after.source_quote: Prescreening is required for this major.

before.source_quote: Prescreen: A combined package representing Italian, German, French, and English; Two operatic arias in different languages, period styles, and tempi/moods; one may be replaced by a concert or oratorio aria; Four art songs spanning different languages, periods, and tempi/moods; At least one underrepresented creator is strongly encouraged; a living composer is encouraged when suitable for classical singing. Audition: The same combined repertoire package as the prescreen.

after.source_quote: Two contrasting operatic arias and four art songs collectively covering Italian, German, French, and English, performed from memory.

before.source_quote: Vocal Arts applicants must submit prescreen recordings to be considered for an invitation to the final audition round.

after.source_quote: Prescreening is required for this major.

before.source_quote: Prescreen: Three songs: one Italian song from the 17th or 18th century; one song in English that is not a translation; and one choice song in French, German, a native language other than English or Italian, or by a historically underrepresented creator. Audition: The same three-song repertoire as the prescreen.

after.source_quote: Three memorized songs: seventeenth- or eighteenth-century Italian, English in the original language, and a contrasting French, German, native-language, or underrepresented-composer selection.

before.source_quote: Vocal Arts applicants must submit prescreen recordings to be considered for an invitation to the final audition round.

after.source_quote: Prescreening is required for this major.

before.source_quote: Prescreen: A combined package representing Italian, German, French, and English; Two operatic arias in different languages, period styles, and tempi/moods; one may be replaced by a concert or oratorio aria; Four art songs spanning different languages, periods, and tempi/moods; At least one underrepresented creator is strongly encouraged; a living composer is encouraged when suitable for classical singing. Audition: The same combined repertoire package as the prescreen.

Audition: The same seven-piece repertoire as the prescreen; Be prepared to introduce yourself and discuss correct pronunciation and the composer/librettist's contribution to the vocal canon.
